title: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
meta_description: Kisan Credit Card provides farmers with affordable credit at 4% interest for crop production, post-harvest, and consumption needs.

hero.one_liner: Affordable agricultural credit at just 4% interest for crop production, post-harvest expenses, and consumption needs

what_is_it: Kisan Credit Card (KCC) scheme provides farmers with timely and affordable credit for their agricultural needs. The effective interest rate is just 4% per annum (7% base rate minus 3% interest subvention for timely repayment). KCC covers crop production costs, post-harvest expenses, farm maintenance, and consumption requirements.

KCC works like a revolving credit facility — you can withdraw and repay multiple times within the credit limit during the year. PM Kisan beneficiaries can apply for KCC through a simplified one-page form. The credit limit is set based on the scale of finance for crops grown and the size of landholding.

eligibility.label: Who can apply
eligibility.value: All farmers — individual, joint, tenant, sharecroppers, SHG members

eligibility.label: Covers
eligibility.value: Crop production, animal husbandry, fisheries, and allied activities

eligibility.label: Documents
eligibility.value: Aadhaar, land records, crop details, passport photo, existing bank account

eligibility.label: PM Kisan link
eligibility.value: PM Kisan beneficiaries get simplified 1-page application

how_to_apply.title: Visit your bank branch
how_to_apply.description: Go to the bank where you have an account. PM Kisan beneficiaries can apply through a simplified process.

how_to_apply.title: Submit KCC application
how_to_apply.description: Fill the KCC application form with land details, crops grown, and credit requirement.

how_to_apply.title: Bank assessment
how_to_apply.description: Bank assesses your land records and sets a credit limit based on scale of finance for your crops and land area.

how_to_apply.title: Receive KCC
how_to_apply.description: You get a Kisan Credit Card (works at ATMs), a passbook, and a credit limit valid for 5 years (renewed annually).

important_alert.text: To get the 4% interest rate, you must repay on time. The base rate is 7%, with 3% interest subvention given by the government for prompt repayment. Late repayment means you pay 7% or higher.

faqs.question: What is the interest rate on KCC?
faqs.answer: Base rate is 7% per annum. If you repay on time, you get 3% interest subvention, making effective rate just 4%. For loans above ₹3 lakh, rates may be higher.

faqs.question: How much credit can I get?
faqs.answer: Credit limit depends on your land area and crops. It includes cost of cultivation + post-harvest expenses + farm asset maintenance + 10% for consumption. The limit increases by 10% each year.

faqs.question: Can fishermen and dairy farmers get KCC?
faqs.answer: Yes. KCC was extended to fisheries and animal husbandry in 2018-19. Fish farmers, dairy farmers, poultry farmers, and beekeepers can all apply.

faqs.question: Is collateral required?
faqs.answer: No collateral for loans up to ₹1.6 lakh. Above that, banks may ask for collateral based on their assessment.
